message:
fix(core): non-UTF8-safe SQLite sidecar paths + delegate checkpoint to db::pool

Two related cleanups in mcp-agent-mail-core that bake the safer
path-handling primitives the rest of the workspace depends on.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/disk.rs

Add `sqlite_sidecar_path(db_path, suffix) -> PathBuf` that builds the
WAL/SHM sibling path by extending the underlying `OsString` instead of
formatting through `db_path.display()`. The previous pattern,

  PathBuf::from(format!("{}{}", db_path.display(), "-wal"))

is lossy on non-UTF8 byte sequences because `Path::display()` returns
a `Display` adapter that replaces invalid sequences with U+FFFD. On
Unix, file names can contain arbitrary bytes (e.g. paths produced by
filesystems with non-UTF8 locales, or test fixtures that intentionally
include high bytes), and the lossy conversion would silently rewrite
the sidecar path so that `bak.exists()` returned false even when the
file was right there. Build the sidecar by appending the suffix to a
fresh `OsString` so every byte is preserved.

A `#[cfg(unix)]` test
(`sqlite_sidecar_path_preserves_non_utf8_basename`) constructs a
`PathBuf` from raw bytes (`b"/tmp/sqlite-\xFF.db"`) and asserts the
returned WAL path round-trips through `OsStr::as_bytes()` with the
non-UTF8 byte intact.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/legacy.rs

- `backup_db_with_sidecars` switches its sidecar enumeration from the
  lossy `format!("{}{}", db_path.display(), suffix)` to the new
  `sqlite_sidecar_path(db_path, suffix)`. The non-UTF8 paths flagged
  above could previously cause the WAL/SHM copies to silently no-op.
- `checkpoint_sqlite_for_copy` now delegates to the new
  `mcp_agent_mail_db::pool::wal_checkpoint_truncate_path(db_path)`
  instead of opening its own `FrankenConnection`, setting
  `busy_timeout`, and running `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` by
  hand. The two implementations had drifted slightly in their error
  handling and the checkpoint helper had moved into pool.rs as part
  of the broader live-db-vs-archive verdict work below; collapsing
  to one call site means there is a single source of truth for
  "checkpoint a database file at a path".
- New `#[cfg(unix)]` test
  `backup_db_with_sidecars_handles_non_utf8_db_basename` builds a
  `legacy-\xFF.sqlite3` fixture with WAL+SHM siblings and asserts
  the backup directory contains all three files with their original
  byte-exact names.
- New `copy_db_with_sidecars_fails_when_stale_target_sidecar_cannot_be_cleared`
  test creates a `target.sqlite3-wal` directory (not a file) so the
  sidecar cleanup step fails, then asserts the error message includes
  the documented "failed to clear stale target sidecar" prefix and
  the offending path. Pins the contract that copy_db_with_sidecars
  refuses to silently overwrite a target whose stale sidecar cannot
  be removed.
